introduction.p: Identify a variable you might use to represent each quantity. Then identify what units would be most appropriate.

statement.p: Let be the area of a house, measured in .

solution.p: The unknown quantity is area. The area of a house is usually measured in square feet. So well define this variable as:
Let A be the area of a house, measured in \text{ft}^2\text{.}

statement.p: Let be the age of a dog, measured in .

solution.p: The unknown quantity is age, which we generally measure in years. So we could define this variable as:
Let a be the age of a dog, measured in years.

statement.p: Let be the amount of time passed since a driver left Seattle, Washington, bound for Portland, Oregon, measured in .

solution.p: The amount of time passed is the unknown quantity. Since this is a drive from Seattle to Portland, it would make sense to measure this in hours. So we could define this variable as:
Let t be the amount of time passed since a driver left Seattle, Washington, bound for Portland, Oregon, measured in hours.
